mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (Rank IC < 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate exceeding half a percentage point within a quarter signals weakening labor demand, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based demand expansion, reinforcing corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint HOUST and PAYEMS growth periods yield negative or flat 60-day forward returns in > 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion phases, rising industrial production reflects peak capacity utilization and pricing power, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 returns as earnings momentum peaks.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth during late-cycle phases correlates negatively with 20-day SPX returns (Rank IC < -0.05 over any 2-year window).

mechanism_narrative: When headline inflation accelerates beyond a 2.5% annualized pace, markets price in aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive in > 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL annualized growth exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and food services revenues indicate sustained consumer spending strength, which directly supports corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month growth shows no positive predictive power for 5-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: When monthly job creation exceeds 150,000 while the unemployment rate simultaneously declines, the labor market demonstrates robust structural healing, fueling equity risk appetite and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline periods yield negative 60-day forward returns in > 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: In mid-cycle expansion environments, accelerating real GDP growth validates corporate profit margins without triggering recession fears, leading to sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 acceleration during mid-cycle phases correlates negatively with 20-day SPX returns (Rank IC < -0.04 over any 2-year rolling window).
